label: Sikshya five-course sample (Free)
description: One course each for: quizzes, assignments, text+video only, all content types, and a structured showcase. Safe to import on staging or after a reset.

courses.title: Sample: Quizzes & knowledge checks
courses.excerpt: A short read plus a full quiz. Ideal for testing grading and pass scores.
courses.content: Focus: quizzes and questions only. Work through the intro, then take the end-of-chapter check.

chapters.title: What to expect
chapters.content: Read the lesson, then start the quiz when you are ready. You can re-run import to add a fresh copy on another site.

contents.title: How this sample quiz is structured
contents.content: Questions include multiple choice, true/false, and fill in the blank. The quiz uses a passing score and a time limit set in the pack.

contents.title: Knowledge check
contents.content: Answer every question. Submit when you are done.

questions.title: Which of these is a learning management system term?
A passing score is a percentage the learner should meet or exceed.
Fill in: WordPress content is often stored in a __ database.
Which is best for measuring recall on facts?

courses.title: Sample: Practice with assignments
courses.excerpt: Written tasks with points and type meta—no quiz in this course.
courses.content: Focus: assignments. Learners upload or paste work. Use this to style submission flows and review queues.

contents.title: Before you submit
contents.content: Read the brief, then open each assignment. Follow the word limits and use clear filenames if you upload files.

contents.title: Assignment: Reflect on a lesson you teach
contents.content: Write 150–200 words. Describe the learning outcome, one activity, and how you would know it worked.

contents.title: Assignment: Checklist for your next course
contents.content: Create a 5–7 item checklist (bullet list) for publishing a new course. Include at least one item about accessibility.

courses.title: Sample: Text and video lessons (no quiz or assignment)
courses.excerpt: Chapters of reading and short videos only.
courses.content: Focus: text and video lessons. No assessments—use this to preview navigation and the lesson player only.

contents.title: Text: Learning objectives
contents.content: Good objectives are specific and measurable. Write them from the learner’s point of view (what they will be able to do).

contents.title: Text: Pacing a module
contents.content: Shorter steps reduce cognitive load. Group related ideas in one screen, and break long text into scannable subheads.

contents.title: Video: Public domain example (YouTube)
contents.content: This placeholder URL points to a well-known public sample video. Replace with your own embed in production.

contents.title: Video: How learners use the player
contents.content: Test captions, quality switching, and keyboard focus on your theme. The URL below is a second copy for variety.

courses.title: Sample: All content types in one place
courses.excerpt: Lessons, one quiz, and one assignment in a single short chapter.
courses.content: Every core content type: text lesson, video lesson, assignment, and quiz—use this to sanity-check a full path.

chapters.title: Complete path
chapters.content: Go in order. Finish the two lessons, then submit the assignment, then take the short quiz.

contents.title: Text: The flow for this course
contents.content: Each item is short on purpose. Your theme should list them in the course curriculum.

contents.title: Video: One clip
contents.content: After this video, complete the small assignment, then the quiz below.

contents.title: Assignment: One-paragraph plan
contents.content: Write a single paragraph describing how you would add a new lesson to an existing course.

contents.title: Quick check
contents.content: Two quick questions. Pass score is 50% for this sample.

questions.title: A chapter can contain more than one lesson.
The sample video URL is stored in lesson video meta for playback.

courses.title: Sample: Structured learning path (showcase)
courses.excerpt: Two chapters, balanced content, and catalog meta—good default for demos and screenshots.
courses.content: This course combines readings, a video, practice, and checks in two chapters. Pricing and level meta match typical free-plugin demos.

chapters.title: Orientation
chapters.content: Get comfortable with the layout. Short lessons; no grades yet in this part.

contents.title: Welcome: how to use the catalog
contents.content: Categories help learners browse. Use clear titles, excerpts, and difficulty labels so the catalog stays scannable.

contents.title: Onboarding: video place-holder
contents.content: Swap this for your own hosted video or a licensed clip when you go live.

chapters.title: Check your understanding
chapters.content: One assignment, then a quiz, so reviewers see grading paths end to end.

contents.title: Project: improve one lesson
contents.content: Pick any lesson in your own site. List three improvements (clarity, media, assessment). 100–150 words.

contents.title: Module wrap-up
contents.content: Covers the ideas in Orientation and the assignment.

questions.title: A course excerpt is shown in the catalog to summarize the value.
Which is a good first step when publishing sample content?
options: Test on a staging site first
